designObjectives: | The 45-foot tall public art installation, Aspire, was commissioned by Louisiana Tech University and the Louisiana Department of Culture, Recreation, and Tourism. The project serves as both an aspirational totem and a symbol of collaboration, unity and inclusivity. The art serves to invigorate the urban experience while bringing diverse parts of the campus and surrounding City of Ruston together. A purposeful harmonious relationship was created between the art, the landscape, and the people. This new public space is now a campus focal point and serves as a permanent symbol for humanity. | Design Concept: | The project, completed in November 2021, is located at Louisiana Tech University along the east end of the Campus Green. The University has placed a high importance to “welcoming gateways” in the master plan. The historic campus core and new Enterprise Campus come together at this location. Campus leaders have determined this campus confluence would be the perfect location for an iconic work of art. At nearly 45 feet tall, Aspire emerges graciously from the landscape in the form of a cascading fountain. Important to the overall campus master plan, the fountain metaphor is meant to harmonize with another noteworthy public sculpture on campus – the Lady of the Mist. | Type:  Public Art, Sculptural Lighting, Permanent Installation | Techniques:  Procedural design, electrical engineering, 3D pixel-mapping, generative animation, software development, and lighting design. | Materials:  Coated steel pipe, powder coated steel panel, acrylic panel, and integrated linear addressable LED lighting. | Partners: Curtis Pittman, Harold Combs

| The main objectives of the "Bird of Paradise" sculpture in Carrollwood Village Park, Florida, are to strengthen the park's identity and enhance visitors' connection to the surrounding natural world. The artwork serves as a wayfinding device and iconic symbol for park visitors, and is a tribute to the importance of wildlife and ecological sustainability. It also serves to bring awareness to these issues. | Design Concept: | The "Bird of Paradise" sculpture is prominently situated in the SE corner of Carrollwood Village Park, and is a 26-foot-tall tribute to the importance of wildlife and ecological sustainability. It serves as a natural symbol of life and enhances visitors' connection to the surrounding natural world. The sculpture also features animated pixel-mapped LED lighting inside the interior ribs and matching color palette that is highlighted during sunsets. | Type: | Public Art, Sculptural Lighting, Permanent Installation | Techniques: | Procedural design, electrical engineering, 3D pixel-mapping, generative animation, software development, and lighting design. | Materials: | Coated steel pipe, powder coated steel panel, Led Controller, ArtNet, DMX, 12V RGB, integrated linear addressable LED lighting | Partners: | Curtis Pittman

| To demonstrate the versatility of Dupont's new product Corian® by transforming it into an interactive and immersive artwork that brings together music, touch, light, and social interaction. The objective was to showcase the unique properties of Corian® and how it can be used in innovative ways. The artwork was commissioned as a way to highlight the capabilities of the material and its potential for use in the world of art and design. | Design Concept: | Cameo No.3 represents the intersection of music, touch, light, and social interaction, where sound exploration becomes the genesis of art. The piece transforms Dupont's Corian® material into a soft and atmospheric sculpture that allows a play of LED-driven light to glow through, while a multi-zoned sound system plays music through the piece via three touch-activated podiums so that individuals can conduct their experience by way of music and light. The underlying concept is united by the notion of blending advanced materials and technology with nature, warmth and human touch. | Type: | Interactive Lighting Sculpture, Audio-Reactive, Semi Permanent | Techniques: | The design and fabrication of Cameo No.3 were developed in collaboration with ZGF Architecture and utilized thermoformed and CNC routed spires. The piece features a multi-zoned sound system, LED-driven light, and capacitive touch interaction. The sound system is audio reactive, allowing the light and sound to change in response to touch. | Materials: | Front-milled and thermoformed acrylic, painted steel pipe, generative animated lighting, generative soundscape, and spatial audio. | Partners: | ZGF Architects,  Piano Push Play,  DotDotDash,  DuPont

| The design objectives for Bird Song were to create a permanent lighting installation that served as a wayfinding device and iconic symbol for guests of The Nines, a luxury hotel in Portland, Oregon. The artwork was intended to be a tribute to wildlife and ecological sustainability, and to bring awareness to these issues through its unique form and the use of generative soundscapes incorporating Pacific Northwest bird chirps. Additionally, the installation was designed to be interactive, allowing The Nines hotel to change the color, chirp frequency, volume, and randomness of the lights and soundscapes through a custom iPad application. | Design Concept: | The design concept for Bird Song is centered around the idea of representing sound physically through the use of 35-foot glass blown sculptural strands modeled after the sonogram of a repeated bird song. The sculpture is hung with aircraft cable through the middle of each section of hand-blown glass and custom clips hold 3-foot sections of glass in place. LED strips were fabricated to allow double-sided emission and extend voltage transmission length, and addressable lighting technology was used to animate the full vertical height of the chandelier. The iPad control system communicates using OSC over the local network and is remotely controllable. | Type:  I | nteractive Lighting, Audio Reactive Lighting, Permanent Installation | Technique: | generative soundscapes, 3D modeling, custom fabrication, animated LED lighting, pixel mapping, and software development | Materials: | glass blown sculptural strands, aircraft cable, Laser cut stainless, addressable LED strips, iPad | Location: | The Nines Hotel - Portland, Oregon. | Partners: | Melody Owen, DotDotDash

designObjectives: | The goal of this project was to create an art wall treatment that would converge the core values of the University of Santa Clara with the new science and technology building and curriculum. The mural was designed to be located in the entrance of the state-of-the-art facility, which was designed and managed by ZGF architects. The "Converging Influences" art tech wall was commissioned by the University of Santa Clara's Campus for Innovation and Discovery to create an interactive and visually striking display that unites the core values of the university with the aesthetic and intellectual atmosphere of the campus. The wall was installed on October 15, 2021. | Design Concept: | The custom-fabricated 3D wall incorporates LED pixel mapping technology, custom-made software, and generative animated lighting control and playback to create an interactive and visually striking display. The wall was designed with the intention of uniting the core values of the university with the aesthetic and intellectual atmosphere of the Campus for Innovation and Discovery. The mural features a 3D surface that was panelized and milled using Corian and Acrylic. The names of individual donors who contributed to the university are featured in multiple tiers. In addition to the incredible detail and beautiful finishes, embedded individually addressable LED lighting is used throughout to create intrigue. The lighting is pixel-mapped and animated to help convey the concepts of interweaving science and innovation. The design of the mural was created using a procedural design approach, allowing for a high level of flexibility and adaptability. | Type: | Art Wall, Permanent Lighting Installation, 3D Relief Carving, Mural, Interactive Lighting | Techniques: | Procedural Design, Panelization, CNC Milling, Pixel-Mapping, Generative animation, Software Development | Materials: | Corian, Acrylic, LED lighting, Steel Frame, Plywood Backboard | Partners: | ZGF Architects

The objective was to create an immersive, otherworldly experience that captivates visitors with dynamic lighting and kinetic elements. | Design Concepts: | We transformed the space into a 90's themed lounge with a unique retro-futuristic vibe. The design included the installation of a kinetic DMX winch system that brought an otherworldly experience to life. The entire space was covered with dynamic and animated LED lighting, and the kinetic centerpiece chandelier moved gracefully, dancing in the air. Video panels and custom-made LED lighting were pixel mapped and visualized in 3D space, creating a visually stunning environment. | IKON LOUNGE | Techniques: | Interior Design, Custom Fabrication, Software Development, Kinetic DMX Winch System, Dynamic and Animated LED Lighting, Pixel Mapping and 3D Visualization, Lighting Programming, | Software: | TouchDesigner, GrandMA, Resolume, Rhino3D | Materials: | LED Lighting, Kinetic Lighting, Video Panels, Custom Lighting Fixtures | Partners: | Zfab

designObjectives: | Amazon Music aimed to create an unforgettable festival experience that would break through the clutter and delight audiences at select 2023 music festivals. The objective was to provide fans with unprecedented access to music and artists they love, while also engaging them with interactive elements that enhance their festival experience. | Design Concept : | At Outside Lands, the experience featured a reflective exterior and a custom mural by Bay Area artist Chaz Bear. Fans passed through a colorful, astera-lit hallway into a music-filled space that took over the 3-day festival. The space included meet & greet sessions with artists and interviews hosted by Bimma Williams, enhancing fan engagement. At Stagecoach, attendees enjoyed the official festival livestream in a striking, technicolor build with interactive touchpoints throughout the space, including exclusive merch giveaways, artist meet & greets, VIP pass giveaways, and an Amazon Music LIVE photobooth. | Type: | Interactive Media Experience, Event Production, Festival Activation | Techniques: | Reflective Exterior Design, Custom Mural Creation, Animated LED Lighting, Interactive Touchpoints, AR Moments with Free-standing LED Walls, Real-time Physics Simulations, Interactive Programming | Materials: | LED Video Panels, Reflective Materials, Astera Lights, Custom Murals, TouchDesigner Software, Microsoft Kinect Azure | Partners: Kamp Grizzly, Hawthorne Industries, Sharing Box

designObjectives: | The goal of this project was to create a custom fabricated set piece for Adriatique's upcoming worldwide tour, "X Future," that would serve as the central element in the stage design. The set piece was designed to be easily crated, toured, rigged, pixel mapped, laser mapped, and flown in any orientation. | Design Concept: | The custom-fabricated set piece was engineered to meet the highest safety standards in California, United States and shipped with all appropriate drawings and load specifications and engineering stamps. The set piece was fabricated using custom edge-lit DMX addressable LEDs that outline and are recessed inside of the structural arms of the X. The highly detailed build drawings were concepted in Rhino3D and finalized in Solid Works. The X is composed of 4 identical armatures and an interchangeable exterior skinning system, allowing for maximum modularity and flexibility. The set piece was built from aluminum to maximize the strength to weight ratio, with extensive rigging points available on each face to allow for unlimited rigging options. | Type: | Custom Scenic, Fabrication, Stage Design, Concert, Event Production | Techniques: | Projection Mapping, Interactive Software Development, Lighting Design, Environmental Design, Custom Fabrication, 3D scanning, Holographic Displays, 3D World Creation. | Materials: | Aluminum,  DMX addressable LEDs,  Silicone Differ, Hardened Steel | Partners: | SETUP.DESIGN

texts: Interactive Media Experience, Event Production, Festival Activation

Amazon Music aimed to create an unforgettable festival experience that would break through the clutter and delight audiences at select 2023 music festivals. The objective was to provide fans with unprecedented access to music and artists they love, while also engaging them with interactive elements that enhance their festival experience.

At Outside Lands, the experience featured a reflective exterior and a custom mural by Bay Area artist Chaz Bear. Fans passed through a colorful, astera-lit hallway into a music-filled space that took over the 3-day festival. The space included meet & greet sessions with artists and interviews hosted by Bimma Williams, enhancing fan engagement. At Stagecoach, attendees enjoyed the official festival livestream in a striking, technicolor build with interactive touchpoints throughout the space, including exclusive merch giveaways, artist meet & greets, VIP pass giveaways, and an Amazon Music LIVE photobooth.
Interactive Media Experience, Event Production, Festival Activation
Reflective Exterior Design, Custom Mural Creation, Animated LED Lighting, Interactive Touchpoints, AR Moments with Free-standing LED Walls, Real-time Physics Simulations, Interactive Programming
LED Video Panels, Reflective Materials, Astera Lights, Custom Murals, TouchDesigner Software, Microsoft Kinect Azure
Partners: Kamp Grizzly, Hawthorne Industries, Sharing Box
TYPE: ART BASEL MIAMI Experiential Event
TYPE: ART BASEL MIAMI Experiential Event

Create a real-world space for the NFT Miami event that brings the AKU brand to life, featuring a projection-mapped environment in the main room, an interactive subway lobby, a merch room with 3D holographic displays, and a body scanner for creating and minting digital avatars. Host panel discussions, live music performances, and a patio bar for attendees. Custom fabrication of a large-scale helmet installation, as well as exterior signage, was also included in the project.

The AKU World: Experiential Event is centered around the character of Aku, a digital explorer on a mission to prove that no dream is too big and no obstacle is too large. Guests are invited to explore the three virtual worlds created for the experience, including the opportunity to control the helmet installation, choose their "Inner Astronaut," and create and mint their own digital avatar through a 4D scan. The projections and lighting in the space are also linked to the musical performances, creating a fully immersive and dynamic experience.
Projection Mapping, Interactive Software Development, Lighting Design, Environmental Design, Custom Fabrication, 3D scanning, Holographic Displays, 3D World Creation.
Projectors, Blender, Touch Designer, Figma, Unreal Engine, Tiled TV Displays, Portal Displays, Custom Scenic Fabrication, LED Lighting

The goal of this project was to create a custom fabricated set piece for Adriatique's upcoming worldwide tour, "X Future," that would serve as the central element in the stage design. The set piece was designed to be easily crated, toured, rigged, pixel mapped, laser mapped, and flown in any orientation.

The custom-fabricated set piece was engineered to meet the highest safety standards in California, United States and shipped with all appropriate drawings and load specifications and engineering stamps. The set piece was fabricated using custom edge-lit DMX addressable LEDs that outline and are recessed inside of the structural arms of the X. The highly detailed build drawings were concepted in Rhino3D and finalized in Solid Works. The X is composed of 4 identical armatures and an interchangeable exterior skinning system, allowing for maximum modularity and flexibility. The set piece was built from aluminum to maximize the strength to weight ratio, with extensive rigging points available on each face to allow for unlimited rigging options.
Custom Scenic, Fabrication, Stage Design, Concert, Event Production
Projection Mapping, Interactive Software Development, Lighting Design, Environmental Design, Custom Fabrication, 3D scanning, Holographic Displays, 3D World Creation.
Aluminum,  DMX addressable LEDs,  Silicone Differ, Hardened Steel

The challenge was to create a one-of-kind event where attendees become creators in an immersive environment that both empowers the inner creative and drives back to adidas and Urban Outﬁtters.   We ﬁnalized a physical VR station design that tied back to the campaign and Urban Outﬁtters retail aesthetic, and spatially laid out the environment and user journey. We devised the full-stack system that would be built which included an app layer on top of a custom virtual reality tool, a custom video pipeline, projection, visual performance and lighting design

Created for Journey’s and ADIDAS, this 40’ tall structure was skinned with a blank canvas and outfitted with a custom built paint distribution device.   Each participant was given the chance to control the robotic paint dripper and release 5 different colors of paint.   Participants simply had to press a button for their selections and auto magically a paint would begin flowing.

The goal of this project was to reimagine a classic Portland location, formerly known as Saucebox, and transform it into a 90's themed lounge with a retro-futuristic twist. The objective was to create an immersive, otherworldly experience that captivates visitors with dynamic lighting and kinetic elements.

We transformed the space into a 90's themed lounge with a unique retro-futuristic vibe. The design included the installation of a kinetic DMX winch system that brought an otherworldly experience to life. The entire space was covered with dynamic and animated LED lighting, and the kinetic centerpiece chandelier moved gracefully, dancing in the air. Video panels and custom-made LED lighting were pixel mapped and visualized in 3D space, creating a visually stunning environment.
Interior Design, Custom Fabrication, Software Development, Kinetic DMX Winch System, Dynamic and Animated LED Lighting, Pixel Mapping and 3D Visualization, Lighting Programming,

The goal of this project was to create an art wall treatment that would converge the core values of the University of Santa Clara with the new science and technology building and curriculum. The mural was designed to be located in the entrance of the state-of-the-art facility, which was designed and managed by ZGF architects. The "Converging Influences" art tech wall was commissioned by the University of Santa Clara's Campus for Innovation and Discovery to create an interactive and visually striking display that unites the core values of the university with the aesthetic and intellectual atmosphere of the campus. The wall was installed on October 15, 2021.

The custom-fabricated 3D wall incorporates LED pixel mapping technology, custom-made software, and generative animated lighting control and playback to create an interactive and visually striking display. The wall was designed with the intention of uniting the core values of the university with the aesthetic and intellectual atmosphere of the Campus for Innovation and Discovery. The mural features a 3D surface that was panelized and milled using Corian and Acrylic. The names of individual donors who contributed to the university are featured in multiple tiers. In addition to the incredible detail and beautiful finishes, embedded individually addressable LED lighting is used throughout to create intrigue. The lighting is pixel-mapped and animated to help convey the concepts of interweaving science and innovation. The design of the mural was created using a procedural design approach, allowing for a high level of flexibility and adaptability.
Art Wall, Permanent Lighting Installation, 3D Relief Carving, Mural, Interactive Lighting
Procedural Design, Panelization, CNC Milling, Pixel-Mapping, Generative animation, Software Development
Corian, Acrylic, LED lighting, Steel Frame, Plywood Backboard
Santa Clara University, Santa Clara, CA
DuPont Corian®, Acrylic, Brass, Generative Animated Lighting

Converging Influences is a custom-fabricated 3D art tech wall that was commissioned by the University of Santa Clara's Campus for Innovation and Discovery and was installed on October 15, 2021. The project incorporates LED pixel mapping technology, custom-made software, and generative animated lighting control and playback to create an interactive and visually striking display. The wall was designed with the intention of uniting the core values with the aesthetic and intellectual atmosphere of the University of Santa Clara's Campus for Innovation and Discovery.

The design objectives for Bird Song were to create a permanent lighting installation that served as a wayfinding device and iconic symbol for guests of The Nines, a luxury hotel in Portland, Oregon. The artwork was intended to be a tribute to wildlife and ecological sustainability, and to bring awareness to these issues through its unique form and the use of generative soundscapes incorporating Pacific Northwest bird chirps. Additionally, the installation was designed to be interactive, allowing The Nines hotel to change the color, chirp frequency, volume, and randomness of the lights and soundscapes through a custom iPad application.

The design concept for Bird Song is centered around the idea of representing sound physically through the use of 35-foot glass blown sculptural strands modeled after the sonogram of a repeated bird song. The sculpture is hung with aircraft cable through the middle of each section of hand-blown glass and custom clips hold 3-foot sections of glass in place. LED strips were fabricated to allow double-sided emission and extend voltage transmission length, and addressable lighting technology was used to animate the full vertical height of the chandelier. The iPad control system communicates using OSC over the local network and is remotely controllable.
nteractive Lighting, Audio Reactive Lighting, Permanent Installation
generative soundscapes, 3D modeling, custom fabrication, animated LED lighting, pixel mapping, and software development

The 45-foot tall public art installation, Aspire, was commissioned by Louisiana Tech University and the Louisiana Department of Culture, Recreation, and Tourism. The project serves as both an aspirational totem and a symbol of collaboration, unity and inclusivity. The art serves to invigorate the urban experience while bringing diverse parts of the campus and surrounding City of Ruston together. A purposeful harmonious relationship was created between the art, the landscape, and the people. This new public space is now a campus focal point and serves as a permanent symbol for humanity.

The project, completed in November 2021, is located at Louisiana Tech University along the east end of the Campus Green. The University has placed a high importance to “welcoming gateways” in the master plan. The historic campus core and new Enterprise Campus come together at this location. Campus leaders have determined this campus confluence would be the perfect location for an iconic work of art. At nearly 45 feet tall, Aspire emerges graciously from the landscape in the form of a cascading fountain. Important to the overall campus master plan, the fountain metaphor is meant to harmonize with another noteworthy public sculpture on campus – the Lady of the Mist.
Type:  Public Art, Sculptural Lighting, Permanent Installation
Techniques:  Procedural design, electrical engineering, 3D pixel-mapping, generative animation, software development, and lighting design.
Materials:  Coated steel pipe, powder coated steel panel, acrylic panel, and integrated linear addressable LED lighting.

Aspire is a 45-foot tall public art installation commissioned by Louisiana Tech University and the Louisiana Department of Culture, Recreation, and Tourism. The project, completed in November 2021, serves as both an aspirational totem and a symbol of collaboration, unity, and inclusivity and made of coated steel pipe, powder coated steel panel, acrylic panel, and integrated LED lighting. The artist responsible for the project is Curtis Pittman and Nathan Jenkins served as the lighting design creative technologist.

The main objectives of the "Bird of Paradise" sculpture in Carrollwood Village Park, Florida, are to strengthen the park's identity and enhance visitors' connection to the surrounding natural world. The artwork serves as a wayfinding device and iconic symbol for park visitors, and is a tribute to the importance of wildlife and ecological sustainability. It also serves to bring awareness to these issues.

The "Bird of Paradise" sculpture is prominently situated in the SE corner of Carrollwood Village Park, and is a 26-foot-tall tribute to the importance of wildlife and ecological sustainability. It serves as a natural symbol of life and enhances visitors' connection to the surrounding natural world. The sculpture also features animated pixel-mapped LED lighting inside the interior ribs and matching color palette that is highlighted during sunsets.

This sculpture is located in a community park in Carrollwood Village, Florida, and is 27 feet tall. It serves as a wayfinding device and iconic symbol for park visitors. Its unique form strengthens the park's identity and enhances visitors' connection to the surrounding natural world. The artwork is a tribute to wildlife and ecological sustainability and brings awareness to these issues. It also has animated pixel-mapped LED lighting inside the interior ribs and matching color palette that is highlighted during sunsets.

To demonstrate the versatility of Dupont's new product Corian® by transforming it into an interactive and immersive artwork that brings together music, touch, light, and social interaction. The objective was to showcase the unique properties of Corian® and how it can be used in innovative ways. The artwork was commissioned as a way to highlight the capabilities of the material and its potential for use in the world of art and design.

Built for Jeep at COMPLEXCon, this robot wandered the convention floor and  could be controlled by an XBOX remote from the front seat of the latest Jeep Compass.   Participants could drive the bot while chatting and sending emojis via 2-Way video chat.   The ChatBot was capable of traveling upto 2 miles from the driver, and had a 10 hour battery life.     We used omni directional wheels to provide a game like driving experience, and proximity sensors to prevent the Bot from collision.   Software:  RaspberryPI, Arduino, Touchdesigner

They then go into the booth to kick a soccer ball that automatically captures the action. Finally this app compiles the two images and sends them a photo ready for social sharing.

CLIENT: Oceanic Preservation Society & Obscura Digital

TYPE: Projection Mapping, Content, Deployment, Software

Oceanic Preservation Society and Obscura took to the sky with a first-of-its-kind live video projection that illuminated the Empire State Building with beautiful, inspiring imagery of endangered species as never seen before. The towering images, more than 350 feet tall and 186 feet wide, and covering 33 floors of the iconic building drew attention to the creatures’ plight against mass extinction. Sia and J. Ralph produced and released a song, “One Candle”, specifically for the event. The show was captured, edited, and prominently featured as the uplifting ending of the documentary Racing Extinction by Academy Award-winning filmmaker Louie Psyhoyos (The Cover).
This first-of-its-kind event generated headlines with over 1 billion impressions in worldwide media coverage.
Join the race to save these species at racingextinction.com

The City of San Francisco celebrated the Centennial of its City Hall building with a festival that culminated with an epic digital illumination experience designed by Obscura Digital. San Francisco City Hall’s façade was transformed into a creative journey through 100 years of history ending with a grand visual finale.

Created for Toyota as a part of the G-Man experiential campaign,  this activation featured a car inside of a projection mapped room. The participants were invited to sit in the car and experience a virtual ride down the rabbit hole.   3D content was created and rendered from the perspective of the passengers to maximize the immersive feel of the content.  Content created with C4D, After Effects, and Mapping with TouchDesigner
LOCATION: Moynihan Station, New York, NY

To create a memorable and engaging event experience for guests at BuzzFeed's 2016 NewFronts, showcasing BuzzFeed's creative process for creating content and demonstrating their ability to meet the needs of their audience. The event space was designed with a purposeful flow and highlighted with large projection screens and individually mapped orbs, creating a visually stunning atmosphere that captivated attendees.

The event aimed to highlight BuzzFeed's ability to feed the hearts, minds, and businesses of their audience by showcasing their creative process through a unique and immersive event experience. The main presentation took place on a custom built stage, which incorporated projection mapping and a range of multimedia design techniques to bring the event to life.
Multimedia Design, UI/UX, Event Production, General Session
Projection Mapping, Stage Design, Lighting Design, Event Production, Live Sound

Toyota decided to activate their Music sub label along side the famous Lollapalooza.   We created an immersive event with live music, a beautiful set of custom created projection mapped content.   The lobby of the venue contains a 3 story tall low-poly art wall that made a great canvas for the content.    Programming and  Content creation via TouchDesigner, C4D, AfterEffect.

An icy igloo that transported guests to the North Pole. An interactive experience allowed participants to create their own unique snowflake through gesture. Once completed, they were prompted to toss the snowflake into the sky and watch it explode on the ceiling of a 360 degree projection-mapped geodesic dome.

The project was to create a bouquet of futuristic code-generated, pre-rendered, and audio reactive dimensional art that was projected onto a fabricated profile of the 2016 Toyota Prius emulating its form and function.  Content and mapping where created in TouchDesigner
